comment: SCHEMA & VERSION DEFINITIONS: This section tells the Azure Resource Manager engine how to parse this deployment file.

metadata.comment: METADATA: Tracks how this template was built. The generator block notes that this JSON was compiled down from an Azure Bicep file.

parameters.comment: INPUT PARAMETERS: Values passed dynamically during deployment so you do not have to hardcode names.

metadata.description: Relative DNS name for the traffic manager profile. This acts as your prefix, creating: <your-name>.trafficmanager.net. It must be globally unique across all of Azure.

resources.comment: CORE RESOURCE: This block tells Azure to provision a Traffic Manager instance.
resources.name: TrafficManager

properties.comment: TRAFFIC ROUTING CONFIGURATION: ProfileStatus is 'Enabled' so it takes traffic immediately upon deployment. TrafficRoutingMethod is set to 'Performance', which automatically routes users to whichever endpoint gives them the lowest network latency.

dnsConfig.comment: DNS CONFIGURATION: Binds the unique prefix parameter from above to the public trafficmanager.net domain. The TTL (Time to Live) is explicitly set to 30 seconds to force clients/local DNS servers to quickly refresh routing choices, allowing rapid failovers.

monitorConfig.comment: HEALTH MONITORING ENGINE: Traffic Manager acts as an active watchdog. It constantly hits the root ('/') path over an encrypted HTTPS connection on port 443. Endpoints are flag-dropped if they do not respond with status codes 200, 201, 202, 301, or 302.

endpoints.comment: ENDPOINT 1 (EAST US): Configured as an External Endpoint. Traffic manager labels this target as geographically situated in 'eastus'. If an East Coast user connects, the 'Performance' algorithm will prioritize sending them here.
endpoints.name: endpoint1

endpoints.comment: ENDPOINT 2 (WEST US): Configured as a secondary External Endpoint targeted at Google. It's mapped to 'westus'. If a West Coast or Pacific user hits the traffic manager, they will skip endpoint1 and be sent here to reduce latency.
endpoints.name: endpoint2
